past_medical_history: Ovarian cyst, incidentally found on ultrasound 2 years ago, not followed up
family_history: Mother: type 2 diabetes mellitus, diagnosed at age 48
No family history of epilepsy or psychiatric illness

social_history.alcohol: Rare social drinking, approximately once per month
social_history.living_situation: Lives in university dormitory with a roommate

neurological_exam.mental_status: Alert but disoriented to time and situation. Intermittently agitated, alternating with episodes of staring and behavioral arrest lasting 10-20 seconds. Speech is pressured and tangential with frequent word-finding pauses and occasional neologisms. Unable to follow two-step commands reliably. Attempted Mini-Mental State Examination scored 14/30, with deficits in orientation, attention, recall, and language. Exhibits paranoid ideation, stating that hospital staff are 'poisoning' her. Short-term memory severely impaired.
neurological_exam.cranial_nerves: Pupils 3 mm bilaterally, equal and reactive to light. Extraocular movements full without nystagmus. Facial sensation intact to light touch bilaterally. Mild right lower facial weakness noted. Hearing intact to finger rub bilaterally. Palate rises symmetrically. Tongue midline without atrophy or fasciculation. Prominent involuntary orolingual dyskinesias consisting of repetitive tongue protrusion, lip pursing, and chewing movements throughout the examination.
neurological_exam.motor: Increased tone in all four extremities with cogwheel rigidity at the wrists bilaterally. Strength grossly 4+/5 in upper extremities, 5-/5 in lower extremities when the patient cooperates; assessment limited by encephalopathy and involuntary movements. No focal weakness pattern. Intermittent choreiform movements of the fingers and wrists bilaterally.
neurological_exam.sensory: Withdraws symmetrically to noxious stimuli in all extremities. Fine sensory testing unreliable due to altered mental status and poor cooperation.
neurological_exam.reflexes: Biceps 3+ bilaterally, triceps 2+ bilaterally, brachioradialis 2+ bilaterally, patellar 3+ bilaterally, Achilles 2+ bilaterally. Plantar responses are flexor bilaterally. No ankle clonus.
neurological_exam.coordination: Finger-to-nose testing attempted but unreliable due to encephalopathy and superimposed involuntary movements; gross dysmetria not evident. Heel-to-shin not performed.
neurological_exam.gait: Not assessed; patient bed-bound due to encephalopathy and agitation requiring soft restraints.
neurological_exam.additional: Prominent orolingual dyskinesias with stereotyped tongue protrusion and chewing movements present continuously. Intermittent choreiform and dystonic movements in bilateral upper extremities. Autonomic instability observed during the encounter: episodic tachycardia to 128 bpm, diaphoresis, and blood pressure fluctuations from 105/62 to 158/96 over a 20-minute period. Three brief episodes of staring with behavioral arrest and loss of responsiveness lasting 10-20 seconds observed during the encounter, raising concern for subclinical seizures.

patient.chief_complaint: Confusion, abnormal behavior, seizures, and involuntary movements over ten days
patient.history_present_illness: This 27-year-old right-handed Hispanic woman was brought to the emergency department by her roommate and campus police after ten days of escalating behavioral disturbance and two witnessed seizures. Approximately two weeks before presentation, the patient experienced a self-limited febrile illness characterized by low-grade temperatures to 100.4 F, headache, myalgias, and fatigue lasting about five days, which she attributed to a 'stomach bug' and did not seek medical care. She appeared to recover fully, but four days later her roommate began noticing subtle changes in her behavior: she became unusually anxious, had difficulty concentrating on coursework, and complained of persistent insomnia despite taking over-the-counter melatonin. Over the next several days, her condition deteriorated markedly. She became increasingly paranoid, convinced that her professors and classmates were conspiring against her, and she stopped attending classes. She began making bizarre, tangential phone calls to family members at all hours of the night. Her roommate observed her making repetitive chewing and lip-smacking movements, which the patient could not voluntarily suppress and did not seem aware of. Eight days prior to presentation, her roommate witnessed a generalized tonic-clonic seizure lasting approximately two minutes, followed by prolonged confusion. Campus emergency services were called, and the patient was evaluated at a student health center, where she was given a dose of lorazepam 1 mg orally and referred to the emergency department, but the patient refused to go. Over the ensuing days, she became more disoriented, had a second generalized seizure witnessed by her roommate six days before presentation, and developed episodes of profuse sweating with fluctuating heart rates palpable by the roommate. On the morning of presentation, her roommate found her minimally responsive in bed with repetitive tongue movements and called 911. In the emergency department, the patient was agitated, intermittently combative, and exhibited florid orolingual dyskinesias and choreiform limb movements. She was unable to give any coherent history. Her roommate confirmed that there was no history of prior psychiatric illness, no recent travel, no new medications, and no known substance use.

background.continuity: Continuous with intermittent periods of diffuse attenuation lasting 3-5 seconds

findings.location: Generalized, maximum over bilateral frontocentral regions
findings.frequency: 1-3 Hz delta activity with superimposed 20-25 Hz beta
findings.morphology: Rhythmic high-amplitude delta waves with overriding fast beta activity creating a brush-like appearance
findings.state: Present in wakefulness and persisting into drowsiness

findings.location: Right temporal onset with rapid bilateral spread
findings.frequency: Three subclinical seizures captured during 40-minute recording, each lasting 30-90 seconds
findings.morphology: Evolving rhythmic theta-alpha activity with superimposed sharp waves
findings.state: Occurred during episodes of behavioral arrest observed by technologist

artifacts.description: Frequent intermittent artifact from orolingual dyskinesias and choreiform upper extremity movements
Bilateral temporal muscle artifact during episodes of agitation

activating_procedures.hyperventilation: Not performed due to inability to cooperate
activating_procedures.photic_stimulation: Performed; no photic driving response and no photoparoxysmal response

eeg.impression: Severely abnormal EEG demonstrating the extreme delta brush pattern, generalized rhythmic delta activity, and three electrographic seizures of right temporal origin. Clinical correlation recommended.
eeg.limitations: Recording limited to 40 minutes; continuous video-EEG monitoring strongly recommended to fully characterize seizure burden and response to therapy

findings.location: Bilateral medial temporal lobes, right greater than left
findings.size: Right: 2.4 x 1.6 cm, Left: 1.5 x 1.1 cm

findings.location: Right insular cortex and right frontal operculum

additional_observations: Ventricular system normal in size and morphology
Normal major intracranial vascular flow voids
mri.impression: Bilateral medial temporal FLAIR hyperintensities, right greater than left, without restricted diffusion, hemorrhage, or enhancement. Additional subtle cortical signal abnormality in the right insular and frontal opercular region. Clinical correlation recommended.

findings: Nonspecific T-wave flattening in leads V1-V2
ecg.interpretation: Sinus tachycardia at 118 bpm with nonspecific T-wave flattening in V1-V2. No acute ischemic changes.

labs.interpretation: Abnormal values: Sodium 133 mEq/L (L), Chloride 94 mEq/L (L), Calcium 8.2 mg/dL (L), CRP 1.5 mg/dL (H), ESR 29 mm/hr (H), CK 262 U/L (H).

special_tests.Oligoclonal bands: Positive (5 bands, not present in matched serum sample)
special_tests.Cytology: Reactive lymphocytes and occasional plasma cells; no malignant cells

csf.interpretation: Opening pressure: 24 cmH2O. WBC: 75 cells/uL (elevated) (4% PMN/93% lymph). Protein: 62.3 mg/dL. Glucose: 64.7 mg/dL (ratio 0.61 (serum glucose 102 mg/dL)). Oligoclonal bands: Positive (5 bands, not present in matched serum sample). IgG index: 0.91 (elevated, normal <0.70). HSV-1 PCR: Negative. HSV-2 PCR: Negative. VZV PCR: Negative. Enterovirus PCR: Negative. West Nile virus IgM: Negative. Bacterial culture: No growth at 48 hours. Gram stain: No organisms seen. Cytology: Reactive lymphocytes and occasional plasma cells; no malignant cells. Anti-NMDA receptor antibodies: Positive (titer 1:256).

output.interpretation: Abnormal values: Serum anti-NMDA receptor IgG Positive (titer 1:128)  (H), CSF anti-NMDA receptor IgG Positive (titer 1:256)  (H).

Pelvic Ultrasound.value: Normal, 3.0 x 2.2 x 1.8 cm, normal follicular pattern

4.2 x 3.1 x 2.8 cm, contains a 2.3 cm well-circumscribed mass with mixed echogenicity including echogenic foci (calcification) and hypoechoic cystic components. Internal vascularity present on Doppler. Appearance consistent with mature cystic teratoma (dermoid cyst).
Normal anteverted uterus, endometrial stripe 8 mm, consistent with proliferative phase
Small amount of physiologic free fluid in the pouch of Douglas

Internal vascularity present on Doppler. Appearance consistent with mature cystic teratoma (dermoid cyst).  (H).

Internal vascularity present on Doppler. Appearance consistent with mature cystic teratoma (dermoid cyst). (H)

signal_characteristics.density: Mixed density lesion with fat attenuation (-50 HU), soft tissue, and focal calcification
signal_characteristics.enhancement: Thin peripheral enhancement, no solid enhancing nodularity

findings.mass_effect: Mild mass effect on adjacent left ovarian parenchyma

additional_observations: Liver, spleen, pancreas, adrenal glands unremarkable
output.impression: No evidence of disseminated malignancy, lymphadenopathy, or other primary neoplasm. Clinical correlation recommended.

special_tests.Oligoclonal bands: Positive (5 bands, unchanged from prior LP)

output.interpretation: Opening pressure: 23 cmH2O. WBC: 58 cells/uL (elevated, decreased from 78) (3% PMN/94% lymph). Protein: 58.4 mg/dL. Glucose: 67.8 mg/dL (ratio 0.63 (serum glucose 103 mg/dL)). Oligoclonal bands: Positive (5 bands, unchanged from prior LP). IgG index: 0.84 (elevated, mildly improved from 0.91). Anti-NMDA receptor antibodies: Positive (titer 1:128, decreased from 1:256). Cytology: Reactive lymphocytes, no malignant cells.
Abnormal values: Serum osmolality 248 mOsm/kg (L), Urine sodium 53 mEq/L (H).

differential.key_distinguishing: HSV encephalitis classically presents with hemorrhagic unilateral temporal lobe involvement on MRI, restricted diffusion, and positive CSF HSV PCR. This patient has bilateral non-hemorrhagic temporal FLAIR signal, no restricted diffusion, and negative HSV PCR.

First-break psychosis does not feature seizures, movement disorders, autonomic instability, CSF pleocytosis, or a severely abnormal EEG with extreme delta brush. This patient has all of these features.

Requires antipsychotic exposure; characterized by lead-pipe rigidity, markedly elevated CK (typically >1000 U/L), and high fever. This patient has no antipsychotic exposure and CK is only modestly elevated at 245 U/L.
Characterized by elevated anti-TPO antibodies, often with thyroid dysfunction, and rapid steroid responsiveness. This patient has normal TSH, normal free T4, and normal anti-TPO antibodies.
CNS vasculitis causes multifocal infarcts and vessel irregularities on angiography. MRI in this patient shows no ischemic changes or infarcts.

optimal_actions.action: Stabilize the patient: ensure airway protection, establish IV access, place on continuous cardiac and hemodynamic monitoring. Administer IV lorazepam 2 mg if active seizure.
optimal_actions.expected_finding: Seizure termination if actively seizing; hemodynamic stabilization

optimal_actions.action: Obtain urgent bloodwork: CBC, BMP, CRP, ESR, CK, hepatic panel, thyroid function tests, urine drug screen, blood alcohol level, blood cultures
optimal_actions.expected_finding: Hyponatremia (129) suggesting SIADH, elevated CK from seizures, mildly elevated inflammatory markers, normal thyroid function, negative toxicology

optimal_actions.action: Order brain MRI with and without gadolinium contrast
optimal_actions.expected_finding: Bilateral medial temporal FLAIR hyperintensities without hemorrhage or restricted diffusion, right insular/opercular signal abnormality

optimal_actions.action: Perform lumbar puncture with CSF sent for cell count with differential, protein, glucose, Gram stain, bacterial culture, HSV PCR, VZV PCR, enterovirus PCR, oligoclonal bands, IgG index, cytology, and anti-NMDA receptor antibodies
optimal_actions.expected_finding: Lymphocytic pleocytosis, elevated protein, positive oligoclonal bands, elevated IgG index, negative viral PCRs, positive anti-NMDA receptor antibodies

optimal_actions.action: Start empiric IV acyclovir 10 mg/kg every 8 hours pending HSV PCR results, as HSV encephalitis cannot be excluded until PCR returns
optimal_actions.expected_finding: Empiric coverage until HSV is definitively excluded
Extreme delta brush pattern, subclinical electrographic seizures, severely disorganized background

optimal_actions.action: Start anti-seizure medication: levetiracetam 1000 mg IV loading dose, then 1000 mg IV every 12 hours
optimal_actions.expected_finding: Reduction in seizure frequency and severity

optimal_actions.action: Upon confirmation of anti-NMDA receptor antibody positivity, initiate first-line immunotherapy: IV methylprednisolone 1 g daily for 5 days AND IVIG 0.4 g/kg/day for 5 days concurrently
optimal_actions.expected_finding: Initiation of antibody-mediated injury reduction; clinical improvement expected over days to weeks

optimal_actions.action: Order pelvic ultrasound to screen for ovarian teratoma
optimal_actions.expected_finding: Identification of left ovarian teratoma serving as immunologic trigger

optimal_actions.action: Consult gynecologic surgery for teratoma resection
optimal_actions.expected_finding: Surgical removal of immunologic trigger, which improves clinical outcomes and reduces relapse

optimal_actions.action: Implement fluid restriction (1.0-1.2 L/day) for SIADH management with serial sodium monitoring every 6-8 hours
optimal_actions.expected_finding: Gradual sodium correction; target correction no more than 8-10 mEq/L per 24 hours

optimal_actions.action: If inadequate clinical improvement after 2 weeks of first-line immunotherapy: escalate to second-line therapy with rituximab 375 mg/m2 weekly for 4 cycles
optimal_actions.expected_finding: B-cell depletion with subsequent clinical improvement in refractory cases

optimal_actions.action: Admit to ICU for close monitoring of autonomic instability, airway protection, and seizure management
optimal_actions.expected_finding: Safe monitoring environment for potential rapid deterioration including central hypoventilation

critical_actions: Perform lumbar puncture and send CSF for anti-NMDA receptor antibodies
Start empiric IV acyclovir until HSV PCR results are negative
Initiate first-line immunotherapy (IV methylprednisolone and IVIG or plasma exchange) once anti-NMDAR diagnosis is confirmed
Screen for ovarian teratoma with pelvic imaging in this young woman
Start anti-seizure medication for seizure control
Institute continuous EEG monitoring to detect and quantify subclinical seizures
Admit to ICU for monitoring of autonomic instability and potential airway compromise
contraindicated_actions: Diagnosing as primary psychiatric disorder and discharging without autoimmune encephalitis workup
Administering high-dose typical antipsychotics (e.g., haloperidol), which can worsen dyskinesias and precipitate neuroleptic malignant syndrome
Delaying immunotherapy while awaiting final antibody confirmation if clinical suspicion is high and CSF shows pleocytosis
Performing brain biopsy when anti-NMDA receptor antibody testing is available and clinical presentation is characteristic
Rapidly correcting sodium (>8-10 mEq/L/24 hours), which risks osmotic demyelination syndrome in the setting of chronic SIADH
key_reasoning_points: The triad of subacute psychiatric symptoms, new-onset seizures, and orolingual dyskinesias in a young woman is the classic presentation of anti-NMDA receptor encephalitis
Extreme delta brush on EEG is pathognomonic for anti-NMDA receptor encephalitis and differentiates it from other encephalitides and primary psychiatric conditions
A prodromal viral illness preceding neuropsychiatric deterioration is a recognized feature of anti-NMDA receptor encephalitis and should heighten clinical suspicion
Autonomic instability (tachycardia, blood pressure lability, diaphoresis) is a core feature of anti-NMDA receptor encephalitis and necessitates ICU-level monitoring
Hyponatremia from SIADH is a common complication of CNS inflammation in anti-NMDA receptor encephalitis and requires careful management
Ovarian teratomas are found in approximately 40-50% of young women with anti-NMDA receptor encephalitis; removal is essential and improves neurological outcomes
CSF anti-NMDA receptor antibody testing is more sensitive than serum testing and must always be performed; a negative serum test does not exclude the diagnosis
Empiric acyclovir must be administered until HSV encephalitis is definitively excluded by PCR, as delayed treatment for HSV carries significant morbidity and mortality
The family history of autoimmune disease (maternal aunt with SLE) and the previously noted ovarian cyst may represent risk factors and early findings, respectively

metadata.case_variant_notes: Straightforward case variant S04 features a 21-year-old Hispanic nursing student with classic anti-NMDAR encephalitis presentation. Distinguishing features from S01 include left-sided ovarian teratoma, family history of autoimmune disease, slightly lower sodium, and prior incidental ovarian cyst on history.
